tips_en: I recommend starting with the PRISM paper, which offers a broadly applicable post-training framework that repairs the perception-reasoning drift limiting current multimodal models; this foundational improvement is precisely what agents in Claw-Eval-Live and patent systems in PatRe desperately need. Both benchmark papers share a shift from static, output-only evaluation toward dynamic, process-grounded assessment—Claw-Eval-Live via live workflow execution and PatRe via adversarial multi-turn prosecution—revealing severe capability gaps even in frontier models. Together, PRISM provides a training path forward while the benchmarks define the urgent evaluation frontiers.
tips_zh: 建议首先阅读PRISM论文，它提出了一个通用的后训练框架，修复了限制当前多模态模型感知与推理的分布漂移问题，而这正是Claw-Eval-Live和PatRe基准测试中所暴露的智能体能力的核心瓶颈。后两篇论文的共同之处在于均从静态、输出导向的评估转向动态、过程驱动的真实能力检验——前者聚焦实时工作流执行，后者模拟对抗性多轮专利辩论——揭示了前沿模型在复杂推理与执行中的显著缺陷。三者结合，PRISM指明了训练方法的改进路径，而两项基准则定义了亟待突破的评估前沿。
